plan_adherence_notes: The execute commit matches the main planned test-contract work: it tightened HTTP 404/405 exposure assertions to exact structured error payloads, added the over-limit before/after `/v1/accounts/{id}/exposure` regression, and the full 17-test focused slice from the plan passes when re-run. Adherence is not perfect because the branch also changed `backend/server.py` with a service-index constant extraction and an explanatory comment even though the plan described validation and test tightening rather than production-code edits, and there is no retained evidence that the focused slice was run during execution time itself.
missed_questions: Should step 5 be constrained to test-only changes, with no production-code refactors in `backend/server.py`?
reason: The focused backend validation slice passes, but this task is missing `.fleet/tasks/bayes-market-eu0-v2-execute-step-3-execute-step-5/research.json`, so the plan cannot be verified against any recorded research, and the execute commit touched production code outside the planned test-tightening scope.
